description: Perceptual visual principles for EFS Blender scenes. Decoded from analysis of professional audio-reactive VFX creators (UON Visuals, Beeple, Electric Sheep). Each principle maps to concrete Blender parameter adjustments.

principles.name: fractal_detail
principles.description: Multi-scale visual detail from macro turbulence down to sub-pixel noise. The human eye perceives 'richness' when detail exists at 3+ scales simultaneously -- large flame shapes, medium swirls, and fine-grain texture all visible at once.
principles.source: Decoded from analysis of professional audio-reactive VFX (UON Visuals, Beeple, Electric Sheep). Chrome MCP live analysis deferred to interactive session.

fire_domain.vorticity.description: More large-scale turbulence in fluid simulation

fire_domain.noise_scale.description: Minimum noise scale even at preview quality -- more fine detail in smoke/fire

fire_material.density.description: Denser volume reveals more micro-structure in the fire

compositor.glare_size.description: Slightly wider glow picks up more detail in bloom falloff

principles.before_after: Before: smooth uniform fire column with soft edges. After: turbulent multi-scale fire with visible micro-detail at edges, distinct swirl patterns at medium scale, and fine grain texture throughout the volume.

principles.name: emergent_complexity
principles.description: Multiple independent visual systems layered together produce patterns no single system could generate. This is the 'more than the sum of parts' effect -- fire, smoke, lighting, and color grading each add a visual layer that interacts unpredictably with the others.
principles.source: Decoded from analysis of professional audio-reactive VFX (UON Visuals, Beeple, Electric Sheep). Chrome MCP live analysis deferred to interactive session.

audio_mapping.min_simultaneous_mappings.description: Minimum 8 simultaneous audio-to-visual mappings ensures enough independent systems for emergent behavior (already enforced by AUD4-05)

fire_domain.flame_smoke.description: More smoke intermixed with flame adds an independent visual layer with its own motion dynamics

compositor.color_balance.slope.description: Stronger warm/cool split in highlights adds color complexity -- warm reds separate further from cool blues

lighting.rim_energy.description: Stronger rim light creates a visible third illumination layer against key light and fire self-illumination

principles.before_after: Before: fire with uniform warm tones, single light source feel. After: visible interplay between warm self-illumination, cool rim highlights, and smoke wisps catching light at different angles -- no two frames look the same.

principles.name: contrast_darkness
principles.description: The most striking visuals emerge from darkness. Bright effects against pure black maximize perceptual impact. Professional audio-reactive VFX uses 70%+ frame darkness -- the negative space is what makes the bright elements feel powerful.
principles.source: Decoded from analysis of professional audio-reactive VFX (UON Visuals, Beeple, Electric Sheep). Chrome MCP live analysis deferred to interactive session.

world.background_strength.description: Pure black background -- already used in EDM, should be default for all visual-first scenes

lighting.key_energy.description: Reduced key light lets the fire be the primary illumination source, not external lights

lighting.ground_bounce_energy.description: Less ambient fill creates deeper blacks in shadow areas

compositor.color_balance.offset.description: Slightly darker shadow tones than current -- deepens the dark end of the tonal range

compositor.bloom_threshold.description: Lower threshold means more of the fire participates in bloom, but not as aggressive as EDM's 0.5

principles.before_after: Before: fire floating in medium-dark space with visible ambient light wash. After: fire erupting from pure black void, bloom halo visible against darkness, deep shadows give the bright core 10x more visual impact.

principles.name: sync_precision
principles.description: Audio-visual synchronization must be frame-accurate. Latency of even 2 frames (66ms at 30fps) is perceived as 'off' by the human brain. The sync itself should be non-linear: onset events hit hard with instant snaps, while envelopes decay smoothly following natural acoustic decay.
principles.source: Decoded from analysis of professional audio-reactive VFX (UON Visuals, Beeple, Electric Sheep). Chrome MCP live analysis deferred to interactive session.

keyframe.curve_type_onsets.description: Instant snap for beat hits -- no interpolation means zero visual latency on transients

keyframe.curve_type_envelopes.description: Smooth decay for sustained features using AUTO_CLAMPED handles

keyframe.envelope_handle_type.description: AUTO_CLAMPED prevents overshoot on smooth envelope decay curves

audio_mapping.onset_scale.description: Amplify onset events 50% above envelope baseline -- makes beat hits visually distinct from sustained energy

audio_mapping.envelope_smoothing.description: Do NOT smooth audio data -- the analysis pipeline already provides per-frame values at native resolution

principles.before_after: Before: fire gently undulates with music, all movements equally smooth. After: bass hits cause instant fire eruptions (CONSTANT interpolation), while melodic passages produce flowing smooth changes (BEZIER decay) -- the visual rhythm matches the musical rhythm exactly.

principles.name: expectation_violation
principles.description: The most memorable moments in audio-reactive VFX violate the viewer's expectations: sudden darkness before a drop, color temperature inversion on impact, scale shifts. The dynamic_range system already handles breakdowns -- this principle extends to visual surprise through momentary parameter extremes.
principles.source: Decoded from analysis of professional audio-reactive VFX (UON Visuals, Beeple, Electric Sheep). Chrome MCP live analysis deferred to interactive session.

dynamic_range.breakdown_multiplier.description: Even darker breakdowns (8% vs 10% of normal) for more contrast when the drop hits

dynamic_range.drop_multiplier.description: Drops momentarily exceed normal brightness by 20% -- the brief overexposure reads as explosive energy

color_temp.inversion_on_drop.description: When bass onset follows breakdown, briefly invert color temperature warm->cool for 3 frames -- the unexpected cool flash before returning to warm creates a visceral 'hit' feeling

camera.fov_shift_on_drop.description: Subtle shift_x on bass drops (2% of sensor width) -- creates a visual 'punch' without disorienting the viewer

principles.before_after: Before: breakdowns get quieter, drops get louder, all within expected range. After: breakdowns plunge to near-black silence, drops EXPLODE past normal brightness with a brief cool-flash color inversion and camera punch -- the viewer's body reacts before their mind processes it.
